description:
Authors: Yang, Y. and Duan, H. and Abbasi, A. and Lalor, J. P. and Tam, K. Y.
Title: Bias A-head? Analyzing Bias in Transformer-Based Language Model Attention Heads
Conference/Book: Proceedings of the 5th Workshop on Trustworthy NLP (TrustNLP 2025)
Year: 2025
reason: Paper confirmed via ACL Anthology (official proceedings) with matching title, authors, year, and page numbers. arXiv preprint (2311.10395) and workshop details are consistent. Semantic Scholar may not yet index TrustNLP 2025, but ACL Anthology is definitive for ACL-affiliated workshops.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
Yes, this paper exists. Here are the details:

**Title:** Bias A-head? Analyzing Bias in Transformer-Based Language Model Attention Heads

**Authors:** Yi Yang, Hanyu Duan, Ahmed Abbasi, John P. Lalor, Kar Yan Tam

**Venue:** Proceedings of the 5th Workshop on Trustworthy NLP (TrustNLP 2025)

**Year:** 2025

**Publication Details:** Pages 276–290, Albuquerque, New Mexico. Association for Computational Linguistics.

**DOI/URL:** https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2311.10395

The paper proposes a bias analysis framework to identify and analyze biased attention heads in Transformer-based pretrained language models (PLMs), investigating gender and racial bias in models including BERT, GPT, LLaMA-2 (7B), and LLaMA-2-Chat (7B).[1][3]
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

**Yes, the paper exists on arXiv as specified.**

- **Exact title**: Bias A-head? Analyzing Bias in Transformer-Based Language Model Attention Heads[1][2][4][8]
- **Authors**: Yi Yang, Hanyu Duan, Ahmed Abbasi, John P. Lalor, Kar Yan Tam[1][2][4]
- **arXiv ID**: arXiv:2311.10395 (v1 submitted 17 Nov 2023; latest v2 on 16 Jun 2024)[2][4]
- **Venue (conference publication)**: Proceedings of the 5th Workshop on Trustworthy NLP (TrustNLP 2025), pages 276–290, Albuquerque, New Mexico. Association for Computational Linguistics (2025)[1]
- **DOI/URL**: No DOI listed; primary URLs are https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.10395[4] and https://aclanthology.org/2025.trustnlp-main.18/[1]

The arXiv version analyzes bias in attention heads of models like BERT, GPT, LLaMA-2 (7B), and LLaMA-2-Chat (7B), focusing on gender and racial stereotypes, with experiments on debiasing via head masking[2][4]. It was later published in peer-reviewed proceedings[1][6].

The paper **"Bias A-head? Analyzing Bias in Transformer-Based Language Model Attention Heads"** exists and is published in the Proceedings of the 5th Workshop on Trustworthy NLP (TrustNLP 2025).[1]

**Complete bibliographic information:**

- **Authors:** Yi Yang, Hanyu Duan, Ahmed Abbasi, John P. Lalor, Kar Yan Tam[1]
- **Title:** Bias A-head? Analyzing Bias in Transformer-Based Language Model Attention Heads[1]
- **Venue:** Proceedings of the 5th Workshop on Trustworthy NLP (TrustNLP 2025)[1]
- **Year:** 2025[1]
- **Month:** May[1]
- **Location:** Albuquerque, New Mexico[1]
- **Pages:** 276–290[1]
- **Anthology ID:** 2025.trustnlp-main.18[1]
- **DOI:** 10.18653/v1/2025.trustnlp-main.18[1]
- **Publisher:** Association for Computational Linguistics[1]

**Affiliations:**
- Yi Yang, Hanyu Duan, Kar Yan Tam: Department of Information Systems, Business Statistics and Operations Management, HKUST[3]
- Ahmed Abbasi, John P. Lalor: Department of IT, Analytics, and Operations, University of Notre Dame[3]

The paper focuses on analyzing bias in transformer-based pretrained language models by examining attention heads as a major component of the Transformer architecture.[3]
